LHX.sentence: L3Harris slipped a touch, and the thing worth watching is that its profit margins are running thinner than the other big defense contractors — but steady military spending keeps the underlying business intact.

ASML.sentence: ASML ticked up again, which is business as usual — they're the only company on earth that makes a certain kind of chipmaking machine, and demand keeps rolling in.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet stretch — they supply oxygen and other gases to hospitals and factories on long contracts and nudge prices up over time, which is really the whole story here.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft has pulled back sharply from its high, which is unusual for such a reliably profitable company, even as its AI cloud business keeps growing steadily in the background.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher had a good week, and since biotech funding is thawing, more lab orders tend to follow a couple of quarters later for the company that supplies everyone's equipment.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge barely moved — this Canadian pipeline operator works like a toll road for oil and gas, and it's mostly here for the steady dividend, which looks a little stretched right now.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow barely moved but has dropped sharply from its high, which seems overdone for a company whose corporate and government customers keep signing multi-year contracts for its workflow and AI tools.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is lagging a bit even as copper prices have climbed, so the mining and trading giant may have some catching up to do — though that gap has been slow to close.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is well below where it's been trading over the past year, so this is one of its quieter, weaker patches after a big run.

KOF.sentence: The big Latin American Coca-Cola bottler was flat, and it looks cheap here with a decent dividend and a stable Mexican currency backing it.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is paying a very generous dividend right now, though the low price relative to earnings hints those oil profits may be near a peak.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish container-shipping giant that moves goods around the world by sea, rose a bit and is trading for less than its ships are worth, with its logistics arm barely valued at all.

MA.sentence: Mastercard was flat, and interestingly it's priced almost identically to Visa even though its cross-border and value-added businesses are growing faster — people keep tapping their cards more than ever.

ISRG.sentence: Intuitive Surgical, the da Vinci surgical robot maker, is down and off its high on fears about competition, but there's no real evidence hospitals are switching away, and every operation still sells more of their supplies.
